This article about a United States keyboardist is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Steve_Berlin&action=edit).
This article about an American

(born September 14, 1955, in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia")) is an American [saxophonist](/wiki/Saxophonist "Saxophonist"), [keyboardist](/wiki/Keyboardist "Keyboardist") and [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer"), best known as a member of the [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") [group](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble") [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos") and, before that, [Top Jimmy & the Rhythm Pigs](/wiki/Top_Jimmy_%26_The_Rhythm_Pigs "Top Jimmy & The Rhythm Pigs"), the [Blasters](/wiki/The_Blasters "The Blasters"), and [the Flesh Eaters](/wiki/The_Flesh_Eaters "The Flesh Eaters").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Berlin is married and lives with his wife and children in [Portland, Oregon](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon"). Berlin joined the band [Tuatara](/wiki/Tuatara_(band) "Tuatara (band)") as a side project in 1998 on their second [album](/wiki/Album "Album"), *Trading with the Enemy*.

Chris Morris Los Lobos: Dream in Blue 2015 - Page 57 1477308520 "At many of these early gigs, Los Lobos were augmented by another musician who started showing up at their shows, instrument in hand, just for the love of it: the Blasters' saxophonist Steve Berlin. Born in Philadelphia, John Coltrane's longtime home base, in 1955, Berlin was a hard-blowing, R&B- and jazz-savvy saxophonist..."
Timothy Finn ["Making Movies: 'An Amazing Special Band'".](http://backtorockville.typepad.com/back_to_rockville/2012/12/making-movies-an-amazing-special-band.html) Kansas City Star, December 14, 2012

first_paragraph: **Steven M. Berlin** (born September 14, 1955, in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia")) is an American [saxophonist](/wiki/Saxophonist "Saxophonist"), [keyboardist](/wiki/Keyboardist "Keyboardist") and [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer"), best known as a member of the [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") [group](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble") [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos") and, before that, [Top Jimmy & the Rhythm Pigs](/wiki/Top_Jimmy_%26_The_Rhythm_Pigs "Top Jimmy & The Rhythm Pigs"), the [Blasters](/wiki/The_Blasters "The Blasters"), and [the Flesh Eaters](/wiki/The_Flesh_Eaters "The Flesh Eaters").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Berlin is married and lives with his wife and children in [Portland, Oregon](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon"). Berlin joined the band [Tuatara](/wiki/Tuatara_(band) "Tuatara (band)") as a side project in 1998 on their second [album](/wiki/Album "Album"), *Trading with the Enemy*.

Musical artist

**Steven M. Berlin** (born September 14, 1955, in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia")) is an American [saxophonist](/wiki/Saxophonist "Saxophonist"), [keyboardist](/wiki/Keyboardist "Keyboardist") and [record producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer"), best known as a member of the [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music") [group](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble") [Los Lobos](/wiki/Los_Lobos "Los Lobos") and, before that, [Top Jimmy & the Rhythm Pigs](/wiki/Top_Jimmy_%26_The_Rhythm_Pigs "Top Jimmy & The Rhythm Pigs"), the [Blasters](/wiki/The_Blasters "The Blasters"), and [the Flesh Eaters](/wiki/The_Flesh_Eaters "The Flesh Eaters").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Berlin is married and lives with his wife and children in [Portland, Oregon](/wiki/Portland,_Oregon "Portland, Oregon"). Berlin joined the band [Tuatara](/wiki/Tuatara_(band) "Tuatara (band)") as a side project in 1998 on their second [album](/wiki/Album "Album"), *Trading with the Enemy*.

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steve_Berlin&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

As either a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") or producer, Berlin has worked with the [Soul Survivors](/wiki/Soul_Survivors_(band) "Soul Survivors (band)"), [Crash Test Dummies](/wiki/Crash_Test_Dummies "Crash Test Dummies"), [Backyard Tire Fire](/wiki/Backyard_Tire_Fire "Backyard Tire Fire"), [The Beat Farmers](/wiki/The_Beat_Farmers "The Beat Farmers"), [John Lee Hooker](/wiki/John_Lee_Hooker "John Lee Hooker"), the [Paladins](/wiki/The_Paladins "The Paladins"), [Faith No More](/wiki/Faith_No_More "Faith No More"), [Dave Alvin](/wiki/Dave_Alvin "Dave Alvin"), [R.E.M.](/wiki/R.E.M._(band) "R.E.M. (band)"), the [Go-Go's](/wiki/The_Go-Go%27s "The Go-Go's"), the [Smithereens](/wiki/The_Smithereens "The Smithereens"), the [Replacements](/wiki/The_Replacements_(band) "The Replacements (band)"), [Leo Kottke](/wiki/Leo_Kottke "Leo Kottke"), [Sheryl Crow](/wiki/Sheryl_Crow "Sheryl Crow"), the [Act](/wiki/The_Act_(band) "The Act (band)"), [Los Super Seven](/wiki/Los_Super_Seven "Los Super Seven"), [Ozomatli](/wiki/Ozomatli "Ozomatli"), [Rickie Lee Jones](/wiki/Rickie_Lee_Jones "Rickie Lee Jones"), [Leftover Salmon](/wiki/Leftover_Salmon "Leftover Salmon"), [String Cheese Incident](/wiki/String_Cheese_Incident "String Cheese Incident"), [Alec Ounsworth](/wiki/Alec_Ounsworth "Alec Ounsworth") ([Clap Your Hands Say Yeah](/wiki/Clap_Your_Hands_Say_Yeah "Clap Your Hands Say Yeah")), [Raul Malo](/wiki/Raul_Malo "Raul Malo"), [Rick Trevino](/wiki/Rick_Trevino "Rick Trevino"), [Jackie Greene](/wiki/Jackie_Greene "Jackie Greene"), the [Tragically Hip](/wiki/The_Tragically_Hip "The Tragically Hip"), [Great Big Sea](/wiki/Great_Big_Sea "Great Big Sea"), the [Bridge](/wiki/The_Bridge_(band) "The Bridge (band)"), [Nathan Wiley](/wiki/Nathan_Wiley "Nathan Wiley"), the [Dandy Warhols](/wiki/The_Dandy_Warhols "The Dandy Warhols"), [Making Movies](/wiki/Making_Movies_(band) "Making Movies (band)"), [No Te Va Gustar](/wiki/No_Te_Va_Gustar "No Te Va Gustar"), [Brownout](/wiki/Brownout_(band) "Brownout (band)"), [Shinyribs](/wiki/Shinyribs "Shinyribs"), [Deer Tick](/wiki/Deer_Tick_(band) "Deer Tick (band)") and [John Wesley Harding](/wiki/John_Wesley_Harding_(singer) "John Wesley Harding (singer)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steve_Berlin&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** Chris Morris Los Lobos: Dream in Blue 2015 - Page 57 1477308520 "At many of these early gigs, Los Lobos were augmented by another musician who started showing up at their shows, instrument in hand, just for the love of it: the Blasters' saxophonist Steve Berlin. Born in Philadelphia, John Coltrane's longtime home base, in 1955, Berlin was a hard-blowing, R&B- and jazz-savvy saxophonist..."
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Timothy Finn ["Making Movies: 'An Amazing Special Band'".](http://backtorockville.typepad.com/back_to_rockville/2012/12/making-movies-an-amazing-special-band.html) Kansas City Star, December 14, 2012
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** Hanson, Geoff (September 24, 2024). ["The wolves have survived"](https://www.aspendailynews.com/news/the-wolves-have-survived/article_cb3eaca4-7a61-11ef-afd1-931721a5beff.html). *Aspen Daily News*. Retrieved November 3, 2024.
